entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a wide range of fixes across core systems to improve stability and day-to-day usability. Update v1.0.3 includes key fixes to autopilot/AFCS behavior, INS navigation logic, fuel and electrical systems, engine operation, and various cockpit interactions and bindings, alongside tweaks to flight handling, including smoother pitch trim, refined autothrottle behavior, and more accurate climb performance. Furthermore, additional fixes have been applied across visuals, PFB usability, and sounds (corrected callouts and improved audio consistency). The complete changelog follows below: V1.0.3SYSTEMS ⚙️ ART 🎨 PFB📜 SOUNDS🔊 The add-on is available in the MSFS Marketplace for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SoFly’s Cardiff Airport for X-Plane 12 Releasing Apr. 23
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. SoFly has released a new trailer for its upcoming Cardiff Airport (EGFF) scenery for X-Plane 12, marking its first dedicated release for the platform. The scenery is now confirmed to release on Thursday, Apr. 23. SoFly head Calum shared more context in a Reddit post, explaining that the team has spent most of its time on other simulators recently, but now wants to bring more UK airports into X-Plane. Cardiff was chosen because that area “needed some TLC” and offers quite a bit of character for its relatively small size. SoFly followed up with a blog post confirming that Cardiff Airport for XP12 will release on April 23, 2026, and will be available exclusively through the iniBuilds store at launch, with other storefronts planned later. Cardiff has a scenic coastal approach and also has a slightly unusual operational role. In addition to regular scheduled flights from Ryanair and TUI, the airport is also used by British Airways as a maintenance base for parts of its widebody fleet. The airport is recreated in great detail and will be brought up to a modern XP12 standard. As SoFly’s first XP12 project, the team is seeking community feedback, with them suggesting that more X-Plane content could follow if the demand is there. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSimWeekend 2027 to Be Held in Utrecht Apr. 3–4
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. After years at the Aviodrome museum in Lelystad, Flight Sim Weekend has today announced that Utrecht, The Netherlands, will be the new location for its 2027 edition. The organisers have confirmed that FlightSimWeekend 2027 will take place on Apr. 3-4 at Expo Houten in Utrecht, a dedicated exhibition venue just outside the city. According to FSWeekend, the move comes after a period of major growth, with attendance at the 2026 edition climbing to nearly 5,000 visitors, a record for the show, and enough to justify moving to a larger, more professional exhibition space. Utrecht is located centrally in the Netherlands, and the new venue offers significantly easier access for both domestic and international visitors. Utrecht Centraal, the main railway station, is nearby, and Amsterdam Schiphol Airport is roughly 45 minutes away, making travel more straightforward for exhibitors and attendees alike. The Netherlands' national train operator, NS, has direct train connections from Schipol Airport to Utrecht several times every hour, with Utrecht being a major rail junction. The move marks a significant turning point, as Lelystad and the Aviodrome have been closely tied to FlightSimWeekend for years, with the location serving as an aviation museum. Utrecht offers a, perhaps, more conventional trade-show setting, but the FSWeekend team notes a significant capacity boost, expanded floor space, improved layouts, and better connectivity and comfort. According to the venue, Expo Houten offers 10,000 square metres of exhibition space across four ground-floor halls, a congress area for around 350 guests, a wider event space, alongside around 10 meeting spaces. The site is directly off the A27 motorway, including 1,650 free parking spaces, and can also be reached by bus from Utrecht Centraal or Houten station. Read the full press release here. Visit the FlightSimWeekend website here. ‍ More details on 2027 exhibitors, schedules, and tickets are expected to follow in the coming weeks. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A340 Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their A340 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing stability and performance improvements in the WASM environment, along with core aircraft systems fixes. Update v1.0.11 introduces under-the-hood improvements to memory management, data efficiency, and system robustness, improving overall consistency during high-load periods in the simulator. Furthermore, the update also addresses system-level issues affecting day-to-day operations, including corrections to flight deck indications, trim and hydraulic logic, navigation behavior, and MCDU interactions. They have greatly improved the 'add-ons' performance and WASM stability by optimizing memory handling, reducing unnecessary data copying in key systems, lowering transient memory usage, and improving overall efficiency. ​ On top of that, they have refined data access patterns and added additional safety checks across critical memory pathways to guard against invalid states and edge cases. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ V1.0.11 SYSTEMS ⚙️ FIXED Deviation indication when no course tuned in VOR FIXED Trim can be moved without hydraulic pressure FIXED Issues with clearing scratchpad FIXED Fix page formatting issues FIXED Manually tuned navaids not resetting after landing FIXED THS should be +4 deviation on cold and dark load ‍ The add-on is available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $60.84, requiring at least 7.02 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A350 Airliner for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their A350 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024, bringing stability and performance improvements in the WASM environment, along with core aircraft systems fixes. ​ Update v1.2.5 introduces under-the-hood improvements to memory management, data efficiency, and system robustness, improving overall consistency during high-load periods in the simulator. ​ Furthermore, the update includes a comprehensive set of fixes across key aircraft systems, including FMGS logic, flight guidance behavior, navigation predictions, and MCDU functionality. Additionally, they also updated the EFB, sounds, and the aircraft’s model. They have greatly improved the 'add-ons' performance and WASM stability by optimizing memory handling, reducing unnecessary data copying in key systems, lowering transient memory usage, and improving overall efficiency. ​ On top of that, they have refined data access patterns and added additional safety checks across critical memory pathways to guard against invalid states and edge cases. The complete changelog follows below: V1.2.5SYSTEMS ⚙️FIXED Position time manual behaviourFIXED Direct to behaviourFIXED Fix page issueFIXED Issue with Top of climb predictionFIXED TO page not locking in fields when TO phase activeFIXED Fuel & load pageFIXED The aircraft GW & GWCG should be calculated/shown on the ED/PERMANENT DATAFIXED ALT CSTR on NDFIXED Issues with making waypoint-bearingFIXED Deleting pilot waypoints selection exceeds the remaining number if waypointsFIXED T/C marker missing in OP CLBFIXED Altitude selection & flight phase logic during cruiseFIXED Green dot speed mismatchFIXED "NOISE END" box appears on top of the previous "SPD CSTR"FIXED FD Behaviour - Vertical Guidance Timing & Wind Shift Sensitivity ART 🎨FIXED some bolts & other small parts are missing from the landing gear, & some are not attached to anythingFIXED APPR button not animatedFIXED EVA command guard does not openFIXED Ext Model - Sun shades are mounted on exterior window instead of the interior onesFIXED Rudder trim animation inverted EFB📱FIXED Enroute map token loss in flightFIXED Enroute map 403 error due to bad API call SOUNDS🔊FIXED Missing "retard" calloutFIXED No windshear alert ‍ The add-on is available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $81.13, requiring at least 13.56 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Updates Frankfurt Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently issued a major update to their rendition of Frankfurt Airport (EDDF) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing the ground layout around Terminal 3 up to current standards, adding the new taxiway “s”, and many other fixes. Update 1.1.2 brings the ground layout around Terminal 3 to its current state as of April 2026, adds the new taxiway “s”, adds and corrects multiple taxisigns, fixes the terraforming at the intersection between “N14” and “N”, fixes spelling on markings at the de-icing pad 2, added animated jetways to terminal 3, changes the Aerosoft VDGS models to the new “Safedock X” system, adjusts jetway placement to prevent clipping when docked to an airplane, and adjusts the minimum and maximum rotation values on jetways A50-A69. ​ It’s available on the Aerosoft Shop for roughly $24.99, requiring at least 3.7 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft/ToLiss Updates A340-600 Professional for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft and ToLiss have recently issued an update to their newly released A340-600 Professional for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding new functionalities, hardware support improvements, and bug fixes. Update 1.0.2 brings a vast list of new features, including the ability to pin charts in the EFB, an LVAR to inhibit rotary acceleration for hardware FCU interfacing, an option for the SimBrief import to skip filling init page B, SayIntentions CPDLC support, and more. The complete changelog follows below: New Features Fixes & Improvements ‍ ​The add-on is available on the Aerosoft Store for roughly $79.99, requiring at least 18 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SoFly Releases Cardiff Airport for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. SoFly and Sun Studios have released their rendition of Cardiff Airport (EGFF) for X-Plane 12, SoFly's first project for Laminar Research’s platform, following the release of Cardiff for MSFS. The airport is situated just outside Cardiff, in Rhoose, being Wales’ primary airport. It handles both passenger and cargo traffic. In recent years, it served just under one million passengers, placing it around 20th in the United Kingdom. SoFly’s version of Cardiff Airport comes with full 4K PBR texturing across the airfield, fully modelled terminal interiors, and a detailed main terminal building. Also included are some general aviation hangars, the fire station, and other custom airside objects. Notably, British Airways’ maintenance facility is modelled in detail, coming with static aircraft and animated hangar elements that change throughout the day. It also comes with weather and seasonal effects built in, winter textures included, as well as surface reflections and water accumulation during wet conditions, using XP12’s weather system. Ground textures have been custom-made to match the real-world layout, and the airport includes functioning jetways, though you will need to download the OpenSAM library for them to work. SoFly also states that the scenery is optimised to maintain balanced performance. Development of the airport was handled by Sun Studios on behalf of SoFly, with the team highlighting lighting, populated exterior areas, and the use of XP12 SDK. SoFly’s Cardiff Airport (EGFF) for X-Plane 12 is now available on the iniBuilds store, priced at $18.89 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Echo 19 Releases Cessna 208 Precision Audio Pack for MSFS 2020/MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Echo 19 has just released a new soundpack, the Cessna 208B EX Precision Audio Pack for MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024, intended for the default Asobo Grand Caravan EX. The pack has been built using custom recordings, using more than 12 microphones and recording perspectives. The pack includes new audio for the PT6 engine, rebuilt propeller sounds in 3D, and a more detailed cockpit and exterior soundscape. There are new sounds for switches, doors, compartments, startup, shutdown, engine spool-up, and spool-down, alongside some added cabin ambience and dynamic responses tied to the aircraft’s behaviour. Also included are new flyby sounds and overall richer environmental audio, plus a built-in headphone simulation mode, which can be toggled by clicking “AUX” on the G1000. ‍ ‍ View the full feature list As usual, Echo 19 has included bonus liveries, covering two Mission Aviation Fellowship schemes, Echo 19 house liveries, and additional FedEx, DHL, and SoundsAir liveries. The liveries for MSFS 2024 were created by Rhys Brown. The Cessna 208B EX Precision Audio Pack is available now through Echo 19’s store, Contrail, iniBuilds, Orbx, and the in-game marketplace for MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. A portion of each sale will be donated to Mission Aviation Fellowship. It is currently sold for a promotional price of $13.99 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Infinite Flight Releases Massive 26.1 Update
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Infinite Flight, a long-running mobile flight simulator available on iOS and Android, has recently received one of its biggest updates in years, version 26.1, which has made major changes, based on years of work across graphics, aircraft development, and app systems. The update encompasses multiple major systems, performance improvements, various fixes, new aircraft, and several rewritten areas. One of the biggest changes is a completely rebuilt rendering system. Infinite Flight now uses photometric values when calculating lighting, which gives more consistent behaviour across different conditions. The atmosphere and cloud rendering have been reworked, with the sky now having a softer, more natural look. Real-time shadows have been added to aircraft and ground vehicles, and the infrastructure has been changed to facilitate more advanced weather in future updates. Infinite Flight 26.1 also introduces a new way of building aircraft, now developed in collaboration with third parties. The Piper PA-28 Archer II is the first aircraft produced using these methods, developed with external modelling partners. Another aircraft, the Airbus A330-200, has been added, and this was one of the first aircraft to benefit from all the changes made with 26.1, allowing the developers to update and maintain it more efficiently, rather than relying on large, infrequent updates. This A330-200 comes with a large set of liveries, including Air France, KLM, Qantas, Turkish Airlines, and others. Moving on, the user is now presented with a new Flight Debrief screen when completing a flight, essentially summarising flight data in a format. Things such as landing performance, fuel usage, and distance are all tracked, alongside an interactive replay feature. Improvements have also been made to the so-called AutoPilot+ feature, first introduced with Infinite Flight 25.1, allowing long-haul flights to continue flying, without keeping the app open on your screen. It should now be more accurate in showing the current position of the aircraft. The flight planning feature has also been improved. On the Expert server, a dedicated multiplayer "arena" for more advanced app users, taxi speed limits now apply in appropriate areas, runway incursion detection has been improved, and the ATC toolkit has gotten various fixes, including message handling and in-app schedule access. Infinite Flight 26.1 has added 211 new airports, alongside updates to 48 layouts and 210 existing 3D airports. Below are some of the most notable changes included in Infinite Flight 26.1: Graphics and Platform Aircraft Flight Experience Navigation and Planning Expert Server ‍ Full changelog Infinite Flight 26.1 is available now and can be downloaded from the App Store and Google Play Store for iOS and Android devices. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Flight Panels Releases Stream Deck Profiles for ToLiss A320neo
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Flight Panels has continued its investment into the X-Plane 12 platform, now launching a set of Stream Deck profiles for the ToLiss Airbus A320neo, following the original X-Plane launch in March for the Zibo 737-800X and Cessna 172. As Flight Panels is known for, this plugin allows Elgato Stream Deck hardware to function as a cockpit interface, replacing your mouse and keyboard inputs with touch-screen buttons, rotary dials, and similar. The ToLiss A320neo pack includes two separate profiles, one for the full aircraft and the other for the autopilot. The full aircraft profile features over 600 custom-designed controls across 24 pages. It covers a wide portion of the aircraft, including overhead, comms panel, navigation, lighting, TCAS, weather radar, fire protection, and audio. Both 15-key and 32-key layouts are included. The larger layout supports the Stream Deck XL, mobile variants and Corsair’s Xeneon Edge, while the smaller layout is built for the Stream Deck MK.2 and similar devices. The autopilot profile is designed specifically for the Stream Deck +, leveraging the rotary dials and touchscreen. Here you can change your typical autopilot parameters, including heading, altitude, vertical speed, and also the radio frequencies, with the values updating as the dials are turned. Both profiles are now available through the Flight Panels store and selected third-party marketplaces. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Grinnelli Designs F-100D Super Sabre Now Out for Pre-Order
entry.summary: Sim: DCS. Eagle Dynamics has opened pre-orders for the upcoming Grinelli Designs F-100D Super Sabre, a new Cold War-era aircraft for Digital Combat Simulator. It is scheduled for full release later this month, after a development period of more than three years. The F-100D, sometimes referred to as the “Hun”, is notable in aviation history. Entering service in the mid-1950s, it became the first United States fighter capable of sustained supersonic level flight, a huge step forward over earlier jet models. Over time, its role shifted from air superiority to strike and close air support, especially during the Vietnam War, when it frequently operated against MiG-17s and under heavy anti-aircraft fire. Grinnelli Designs has built a fully interactive cockpit with a detailed external model, along with systems and avionics modelled in great detail. The flight model, tested against real-world data and pilot feedback, aims to replicate the F-100’s demanding handling, including its many quirks. One of the quirks simulated, the so-called “Sabre Dance”, is a low-speed instability phenomenon, caused by aerodynamic and control limitations, something that made the real jet very unforgiving in unsteady hands. Some later upgrades tried to mitigate the issue, including the “Project High Wire” improvements, but the aircraft remained challenging to fly, and this carries over into Grinnelli’s recreation for DCS. On systems, the F-100D features a fully simulated electrical and hydraulic system, detailed engine modelling with compressor stalls and accurate afterburner behaviour, early-generation avionics suites, including TACAN, ADF, and radar warning equipment. The RHAW system, for example, is modelled to detect and interpret various radar threats, providing the pilot with feedback similar to facing surface-to-air missiles. The aircraft can carry four 20 mm cannons, also supports AIM-9 Sidewinder missiles, and can be configured with a wide range of air-to-ground ordnance, including bombs, rockets, napalm, and the AGM-45 Shrike for anti-radiation missions. Also included is the fire control system, which can assist during dogfights and bombing runs. Grinnelli has also included a slightly unusual feature, the Zero-Length Launch, or ZELL. This system provides nothing less than a rocket-assisted takeoff, enabling the aircraft to be launched from a stationary platform, using a solid rocket booster. Hence, you do not need a runway. The concept was first tested in 1958, when an F-100D accelerated to flight speed in about 4 seconds under about 4 G. It never saw further daylight in operation, but is nonetheless included in the Grinnelli Designs version.‍ Key Features of the DCS: F-100D Super Sabre: Pre-orders for the Grinnelli Designs F-100D Super Sabre are now live on the DCS E-Shop, with a 30% discount available ahead of release. ‍ Grinelli Designs provides a "progress tracker" on their website, allowing you to track development. They also have a news blog. ‍ ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: TriTriSim Releases GFX Airliner Effects Add-on for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. TriTriSim’s GFX Airliner has now been released for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, an addon that adds physics-driven atmospheric effects to the sim. As a visual enhancement tool, GFX Airliner adds environmental and aerodynamic effects that the default simulator either lacks entirely or handles only in a limited fashion. TriTriSim says the package uses its own real-time CFD-driven calculations to determine when effects should appear, basing them on live aircraft and atmospheric data. The system reacts to conditions such as humidity, pressure, air density, speed, and angle of attack, with effects appearing only when the environment would realistically permit them. A Cinematic Mode allows these variables to be overridden manually. At launch, GFX Airliner includes a range of effects, many of which have been custom-built for supported aircraft. These include engine cold-start smoke and vapour burn-off, jet blast interacting with rain contamination, water trails from landing gear on wet runways, wingtip and flap vortices, engine intake condensation, wing surface condensation, wing fade through cloud layers, volumetric contrails, and AI traffic contrails. The addon runs natively through WebAssembly, with no external application required, and installs via drag-and-drop. It is compatible with the Asobo A320neo, FlyByWire A320neo/A380, Fenix family, a range of iniBuilds airliners, PMDG’s jet fleet, TFDi’s MD11, the ToLiss A340 and iFly 737 MAX. Unsupported aircraft use a generic effects implementation. TriTriSim notes that GFX Airliner is fully compatible with third-party weather engines, including ActiveSky, as the add-on does not interfere with weather injection. ‍ GFX Airliner is available now for an introductory price of $14.99, before increasing to its regular $19.99 price at a later date. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSimExpo 2026 to Reveal First Exhibitors Today
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. FlightSimExpo 2026 will reveal its first batch of confirmed exhibitors later today, with FSA set to premiere the announcement via the FSElite YouTube-channel at 2015Z. FlightSimExpo is one of the largest flight simulation conventions in the world, with them welcoming more than 5,000 attendees across previous events in Las Vegas, Orlando, San Diego, and Houston. The event is organised by Flight Simulation Association (FSA), a community organisation for both newcomers and veterans. According to FSA, more than 60 exhibitors have already committed to this year’s event, with the first round of names to be unveiled during the livestream. You will be able to watch the reveal live and follow along in chat as the names are revealed. ‍ Microsoft has already confirmed that the MSFS team will attend the event. Beyond the exhibits, FlightSimExpo is offering a mixed bucket of optional Tours and Experiences for visitors to attend. These paid add-ons provide access to additional aviation tours, classes, and social events around Saint Paul. ‍ Thursday, June 11 Friday, June 12 Saturday, June 13 Sunday, June 14 Read more about what’s happening at FlightSimExpo 2026 here ‍ All these activities require separate registration, may be subject to capacity or operational limitations, and can be changed or cancelled free of charge until May 15. They are available to book through the FlightSimExpo website. Many activities have limited availability, with several already sold out. ‍ Book Tours & Experiences here ‍ flightsimexpo.com/schedule If you are still undecided about attending, ticket prices will increase on May 1, so it would be smart to book soon! ‍ Buy tickets for FlightSimExpo 2026 Again, the first exhibitor reveal will go live later today at 2015Z. More announcements are expected as the event draws closer. FlightSimExpo 2026 runs Friday, Jun. 12 to Sunday, Jun. 14 2026 ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSimExpo 2026 Announces 60+ Confirmed Exhibitors
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Flight Simulation Association has, a few moments ago, revealed the first lineup of exhibitors for FlightSimExpo 2026, with more than 60 already committed to this year’s event in Saint Paul. These exhibitors are the various companies, communities, personas and organisations who will have an exhibition booth or otherwise be present at the event, showcasing their work to the attending visitors. Revealed during a livestream premiere with FSElite, the initial partner list pretty varied, with hardware manufacturers, software developers, training organisations, online communities, media creators, and more, present at the event. According to FSA organiser Phil Coyle, this year’s exhibitor list has been put together to represent an even wider range of the community, with a broader cross-section of the simulator space on show. ‍ ‍ Among the confirmed exhibitors are several hardware brands, including Honeycomb Aeronautical, Thrustmaster, Virtual Fly, WINCTRL, and Pimax, alongside software and add-on developers such as Aerosoft, FlyByWire Simulations, Grinnelli Designs, iniBuilds, Navigraph, SayIntentions.AI, TFDi Design, and Miltech Simulations. GeoFS is set to showcase GeoFS 4.0, a new version of their browser-based flight simulator, and KatiePilot's efbX will make their first known public hands-on appearance at the event, showcasing their upcoming EFB addon. Thrustmaster is teasing “big news” for their #FSExpoFriday stage presentation, and Microsoft has also previously confirmed that the MSFS team will attend the event. ‍ ‍ Exhibitor registration will remain open until May 29, so expect more exhibitors to be announced in the coming weeks. ‍ Those wanting to become an exhibitor partner with FlightSimExpo 2026 can read more here. FlightSimExpo is also doubling down on military and combat simulation this year. Flight Simulation Association says attendees can expect a much improved DCS presence on the show floor, including at the WINCTRL booth, and Grinnelli Designs will host the new FSExpo 2026 Combat Arena. The Combat Arena is described as an interactive competition and learning space designed around combat and military aviation-themed activities. Honeycomb Aeronautical is expected to bring one of the largest booths at the event, and SKALARKI will return with multiple full-scale cockpit shells, including their A320 and A330 simulators. New exhibitor PXN is also set to debut new yoke and stick hardware. Parallel 42 will return as Platinum Sponsor and will once again host the //42 Exhibitors Lounge, a networking and hospitality area for developers and exhibitors. ‍ Read more about the exhibitors for FlightSimExpo 2026 here Moving on, more than 20 developers are expected to make new announcements during #FSExpoFriday, the first official day of the event, during the presentation and seminar block before the exhibit hall is opened. The main exhibit hall then runs through Saturday and Sunday, alongside seminars, panels, and workshops. ‍ Read more about the FlightSimExpo 2026 experience here ‍ ‍flightsimexpo.com/schedule ‍ Beyond the show itself, there’s several excursions, tours and experiences that visitors can book separately, everything from a visit to MSP International Airport, to Restaurants and Mall of America. ‍ Book Tours & Experiences here For those still considering attending, FlightSimExpo 2026 takes place from June 12 to June 14 at the Saint Paul RiverCentre, around 15 minutes from Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport. Ticket prices begin at $85 for full weekend across, with single-day tickets also available, though prices are will increase on May 1. FSExpo offers discounted rates for several nearby hotels, including the InterContinental Saint Paul Riverfront, the primary partner hotel. FlightSimExpo 2026 runs Friday, Jun. 12 to Sunday, Jun. 14 2026 ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Releases High Detail Update for the 737-900
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has recently issued an update to their Boeing 737-900, bringing it up to the new MSFS 2024 standards through the “high detail update”, already available on the -700 and -600, greatly improving the flight deck, cabin, and externals. The “high detail” update substantially improves the aircraft’s model, adding high-detail tweaks to the operating access panels, ground equipment, landing gear bays, lighting, textures, and more. They have also rolled out small updates to the -600 and -700 to keep all of them on par as far as the “high detail” improvements go. ​ On another note, the Operations Center 2.0 will reach end of life in late May, not impacting MSFS users at all, but FSX and P3D users will have to manually back up the liveries they wish to keep, since they will no longer be available. They are putting great effort into WASM crash mitigation, and it is currently their number 1 strategic objective for the development team. There’s an update undergoing testing that is the first of maybe two or three updates specifically targeted toward reducing WASM crashes. ​ The add-on is available on their store for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍
